text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers check each other’s code before it goes live, they can catch bugs early, before these issues become costly or time-consuming to fix. Beyond finding errors, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within a team. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while everyone gains a better understanding of different parts of the project. This collaborative process helps spread skills and builds a shared sense of ownership. Ultimately, consistent code reviews improve team quality by encouraging better coding standards, reducing mistakes, and creating a culture of continuous learning and improvement.
